title: Urban Heat Island
description: What are effective mitigation strategies for reducing urban heat island effects in dense city neighborhoods?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly and policy-oriented sources that discuss proven techniques—such as green roofs, reflective surfaces, urban forestry, and zoning reforms—to lower ambient temperatures in densely built environments. Relevant documents should describe the methods, provide evidence of effectiveness, and address implementation challenges in cities.

title: AI Bias Mitigation
description: How can organizations systematically reduce demographic bias in large language models used for customer service?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed information on strategies, frameworks, and best practices for identifying and mitigating demographic bias in deployed LLMs. Relevant documents should describe concrete mitigation techniques (e.g., data balancing, adversarial debiasing, post‑processing), evaluation metrics, case studies of real‑world deployments, and policy or governance guidelines. Preference is given to sources from 2020 onward that provide empirical results, implementation details, or comprehensive reviews.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the key policy mechanisms that have successfully increased renewable energy adoption in European Union member states since 2010?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly and governmental sources that discuss specific policy instruments (e.g., feed‑in tariffs, auctions, tax incentives) used by EU countries to boost renewable electricity generation from 2010 onward, including evidence of their effectiveness, quantitative adoption outcomes, and any lessons learned. Relevant documents should describe the policy design, implementation timeline, and measurable impact on renewable capacity or generation. Sources focusing solely on non‑EU regions, unrelated energy sectors, or generic climate change discussions without concrete policy analysis are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Benefits
description: What are the measurable productivity and well‑being benefits of full‑time remote work for knowledge‑based employees?
narrative: The user seeks credible studies, meta‑analyses, or industry reports that quantify how remote work affects employee output, job satisfaction, work‑life balance, and retention, preferring peer‑reviewed research and recent (post‑2020) data; anecdotal blog posts or superficial surveys are not relevant.

title: Carbon Capture Policy
description: What are the latest U.S. policy proposals to incentivize carbon capture and storage technologies?
narrative: The user seeks recent legislative, regulatory, or funding initiatives in the United States that aim to promote carbon capture and storage (CCS). Relevant documents should describe policy mechanisms, financial incentives, implementation timelines, and stakeholder perspectives, preferably from 2023 onward.

title: Solar Policy Impact
description: What policy instruments most effectively drove solar energy adoption in the United States from 2010 to 2020?
narrative: The user seeks a thorough overview of federal and state policies—such as tax credits, feed‑in tariffs, net‑metering rules, and grant programs—that demonstrably increased residential and commercial solar installations during the 2010‑2020 period. Relevant documents should provide quantitative evidence (e.g., installation capacity growth, adoption rates) linked to specific policies, discuss implementation details, and compare effectiveness across jurisdictions. Materials focusing on other renewable technologies, periods outside the specified range, or purely speculative commentary without empirical support are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What federal and state financial incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks up-to-date information on monetary programs, tax credits, rebates, and grants that support homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant documents should list applicable federal policies (e.g., Investment Tax Credit), state-specific incentives, eligibility criteria, and any deadlines or amounts, while excluding unrelated topics such as general renewable energy definitions or non‑residential programs.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What policy mechanisms have been most effective at increasing renewable energy adoption in developing countries?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on policies that successfully promote renewable energy in developing nations, including government incentives, regulatory frameworks, and financing schemes. Relevant documents should provide evidence of impact, be peer‑reviewed or from reputable organizations, focus on developing country contexts, and preferably be published within the last ten years.

title: Dental Whitening Safety
description: What are the potential side effects, risks, and contraindications associated with at‑home teeth whitening products?
narrative: The user is looking for comprehensive information on health implications of using over‑the‑counter or DIY teeth whitening solutions. Relevant documents should discuss clinically observed side effects (e.g., gum irritation, enamel erosion, tooth sensitivity), identify populations for whom whitening is not recommended (such as individuals with existing dental restorations, gum disease, or hypersensitivity), cite scientific studies, dental professional guidelines, and credible consumer safety reports, and provide practical recommendations for safe use. Sources that only mention unrelated topics such as white‑out correction fluid or general oral hygiene without addressing whitening risks are not relevant.

title: Energy Storage
description: Which large‑scale renewable energy storage technologies are the most cost‑effective and currently deployed?
narrative: The user seeks information on technologies for storing renewable electricity at utility scale, focusing on cost per kilowatt‑hour, scalability, technology maturity, and real‑world deployment examples. Relevant documents should discuss battery chemistries, pumped hydro, compressed air, thermal storage, and recent projects, while excluding small‑scale or residential solutions.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal and state tax credits and rebate programs are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States as of 2024?
narrative: The user is seeking current information on financial incentives that reduce the cost of installing solar panels on private homes. Relevant documents should describe federal tax credits (e.g., the Investment Tax Credit), state‑specific tax credits, rebates, eligibility criteria, maximum amounts, application procedures, and any expiration dates or recent legislative changes applicable in 2024.

title: Carbon Pricing Impact
description: How does the implementation of carbon pricing mechanisms affect renewable energy investments in European Union member states?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly articles, policy analyses, and empirical studies that discuss the relationship between carbon pricing (e.g., carbon taxes or cap‑and‑trade) and the level of investment in renewable energy projects within EU countries. Relevant documents must provide quantitative evidence, case studies, or theoretical frameworks that link pricing mechanisms to changes in investment volume, project pipeline, or policy outcomes, and should be dated from 2005 onward.

title: Reflective Teaching
description: How can teachers effectively incorporate reflective practice into their instructional methods to improve student learning outcomes?
narrative: The user is looking for practical guidance, research findings, and case studies on the use of reflective practice by educators. Relevant documents should describe specific strategies (journaling, peer observation, video analysis, etc.), evidence of impact on teaching effectiveness or student achievement, and discuss challenges and ways to overcome them. Materials that merely define reflection or list unrelated language translations are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Storage
description: What are the most efficient methods for storing energy generated from renewable sources?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive, up‑to‑date comparisons of energy‑storage technologies that can be paired with solar or wind power. Relevant content should discuss the efficiency, cost, scalability, maturity, and real‑world deployment of solutions such as lithium‑ion and solid‑state batteries, pumped hydro, compressed‑air energy storage, thermal storage, flow batteries, and emerging approaches, providing performance metrics and case studies where possible.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the key challenges and successes of implementing renewable energy policies in mid-sized European cities since 2010?
narrative: The user wants detailed, city‑level analyses of renewable energy policy adoption in European cities with populations between 100,000 and 500,000, covering case studies, quantitative outcomes, barriers, and best practices from 2010 onward. Relevant documents must describe specific municipal initiatives, measurable impacts, and policy frameworks; broad national overviews, non‑European examples, or discussions that do not focus on concrete city‑scale implementation are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Mobility
description: How has the rise of remote work affected traffic congestion patterns in major U.S. cities?
narrative: The user seeks recent, data‑driven analyses that quantify changes in traffic volume, peak‑hour congestion, and overall commuting patterns attributable to increased remote work since 2020. Relevant material should include statistical comparisons to pre‑COVID baselines, city‑level case studies, and discussion of factors such as city size, industry composition, and mode of transportation. Reports, peer‑reviewed studies, government transportation datasets, and reputable news investigations that provide concrete numbers or clear methodological explanations are relevant; purely speculative commentary or anecdotal observations without supporting data are not.

title: Urban Heat Mitigation
description: What are the most effective, cost‑efficient strategies for reducing urban heat island effects in mid‑sized cities?
narrative: The user is seeking detailed information on approaches that mitigate urban heat islands, especially those demonstrated to work in cities with populations between 100,000 and 500,000. Relevant material should describe specific interventions (e.g., green roofs, cool pavements, tree canopy expansion, reflective building materials), provide evidence of temperature reduction or energy savings, discuss implementation costs, and include case studies or evaluation results. Sources focused solely on climate modeling without practical mitigation guidance, or on large megacities, are not relevant.

title: Clean Energy Incentives
description: What federal tax credits are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States as of 2024?
narrative: The user wants current information on U.S. federal tax incentives for homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems, including eligibility requirements, credit amounts, and filing procedures. Relevant material should cover the Investment Tax Credit, any recent legislative updates, and practical guidance for qualifying households.

title: AI Ethics Guidelines
description: What are the most comprehensive and up‑to‑date guidelines for ethical AI development and deployment across industry and academia?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative resources that outline best practices, principles, and regulatory considerations for responsible AI. Relevant documents should include detailed frameworks, case studies, or policy recommendations published by reputable organizations (e.g., IEEE, OECD, UNESCO, major research institutions) after 2018, and should address issues such as fairness, transparency, accountability, privacy, and societal impact.

title: Solar Incentive Policies
description: What recent policy measures have European countries implemented to encourage residential solar panel installations?
narrative: The user is looking for up‑to‑date information on governmental actions—such as subsidies, tax credits, feed‑in tariffs, and regulatory reforms—introduced in Europe since 2020 that specifically target homeowners who want to install photovoltaic systems. Relevant material should detail the policy name, eligibility criteria, financial incentives, and implementation status for countries like Germany, France, Spain, and Italy, while excluding broader energy discussions that do not focus on residential solar incentives.

title: Solar Incentive Guide
description: What federal tax credits and state-level incentives are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States in 2024?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive, current information on financial incentives for installing residential solar photovoltaic systems, including federal tax credits, state rebates, utility programs, eligibility requirements, monetary values, application procedures, and relevant deadlines. title: Carbon Pricing Impact
description: How does carbon pricing influence investment decisions in renewable energy projects within the United States?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on the effects of carbon pricing mechanisms (e.g., cap‑and‑trade, carbon taxes) on the financial viability and capital allocation for U.S. renewable energy initiatives. Relevant documents should provide empirical studies, policy analyses, or industry reports that discuss trends, case studies, or quantitative assessments of investment shifts attributable to carbon pricing. Sources focusing solely on theoretical concepts without applied evidence, or on non‑U.S. markets, are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Incentives
description: What federal and state tax credits are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user wants current, detailed information on financial incentives that homeowners can use when installing solar photovoltaic systems, including eligibility requirements, credit amounts, program duration, and distinctions between federal and individual state programs. Relevant documents must describe tax credits, rebates, grants, and eligibility criteria specifically for residential solar installations.

title: Urban Heat Mitigation
description: What effective strategies can cities adopt to reduce urban heat island effects without compromising economic development?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on practical, evidence‑based measures—such as green infrastructure, reflective surfaces, zoning policies, and climate‑adaptive planning—that have demonstrably lowered ambient temperatures in urban areas. Relevant documents should describe implementation details, cost‑benefit analyses, case studies, and measurable outcomes, while excluding purely theoretical discussions lacking real‑world evaluation.

title: Solar Incentives California
description: What financial incentives are currently available for residential solar panel installations in California?
narrative: The user wants detailed, up‑to‑date information on state, county, and utility incentives, tax credits, rebates, and financing programs that apply to homeowners installing photovoltaic systems in California in 2024. Relevant content should include eligibility criteria, application procedures, monetary values or percentage offsets, any expiration dates or caps, and links to official sources. Documents that discuss only solar technology basics, commercial‑scale incentives, or incentives for other states are not relevant.

title: Grid Battery Economics
description: What are the cost‑benefit analyses for large‑scale battery storage systems integrated with renewable energy grids in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking scholarly and industry reports that evaluate the economic viability of deploying utility‑scale battery storage alongside solar and wind facilities, focusing on metrics such as capital expenditures, operating costs, lifecycle performance, revenue streams (ancillary services, peak shaving), and policy incentives. Relevant documents should provide quantitative analyses, case studies, or comparative models that address these factors for U.S. grid projects.

title: Elderly Protein Needs
description: What is the recommended daily protein intake for older adults to prevent or reduce age‑related muscle loss (sarcopenia)?
narrative: The user is seeking authoritative guidance on how much protein older adults should consume each day to maintain muscle mass and function. Relevant documents should include current dietary guidelines, peer‑reviewed studies, systematic reviews, or clinical recommendations (e.g., from the WHO, NIH, ESPEN, or gerontology societies) that specify gram‑per‑kilogram body weight values, total gram amounts, and any nuances such as optimal protein distribution across meals, preferred protein sources (animal vs. plant), timing relative to exercise, and considerations for common comorbidities (e.g., kidney disease). Only sources that directly address protein intake for the elderly population and provide quantitative recommendations or evidence‑based rationale are considered relevant.

title: Carbon Capture Economics
description: What are the current economic challenges and policy mechanisms influencing large‑scale carbon capture and storage projects?
narrative: The user is seeking up‑to‑date information on the financial viability of carbon capture and storage (CCS) initiatives, including capital and operating costs, revenue streams, subsidy or tax credit programs, and regulatory frameworks. Relevant documents should provide quantitative cost analyses, discuss financing models (e.g., public‑private partnerships, carbon pricing), describe policy incentives or barriers, and ideally reference real‑world CCS projects or pilot studies. Sources that only mention CCS in a technical or scientific context without addressing economic or policy aspects are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Housing
description: How has the increase in remote work arrangements impacted urban housing prices and availability?
narrative: The user is looking for analyses, reports, or studies that discuss the effects of remote work on city housing markets, including changes in property values, rental rates, vacancy rates, and migration patterns. Relevant documents should provide data, trends, or expert commentary from the past five years, focusing on major urban areas where remote work adoption is significant. Sources that only address remote work in general without linking it to housing outcomes, or that discuss housing in purely rural contexts, are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Mitigation
description: What strategies can city planners use to reduce summer heat island effects in large metropolitan areas?
narrative: The user is looking for authoritative sources (e.g., academic papers, government reports, case studies) that discuss practical and evidence‑based interventions—such as green roofs, urban forestry, reflective surfaces, and zoning policies—to lower ambient temperatures during hot, rainy summer months. Relevant documents should describe the methods, provide evaluation metrics or results, and address feasibility or implementation challenges in dense urban settings.

title: Historical Divorce Data
description: Where can I locate comprehensive, publicly accessible U.S. divorce records from the 1960s onward for genealogical research?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative sources that provide divorce records (including parties' names, filing dates, and jurisdiction) dating back to the 1960s. Relevant documents include state or county vital records offices, court archive portals, and searchable databases that describe how to request or download these records, any associated fees, and applicable privacy laws. Resources that only discuss general public record concepts, unrelated health plan identifiers, or modern divorce statistics are not relevant.

narrative: The user wants current information on financial incentives that homeowners can use to offset the cost of installing solar photovoltaic systems. title: EU Renewable Policy
description: What policy mechanisms have effectively boosted renewable energy adoption in European countries since 2010?
narrative: The user seeks detailed information on government policies, incentives, regulations, and programs implemented in EU member states from 2010 onward that have demonstrably increased the share of renewable energy. Relevant documents should describe specific policy instruments (e.g., feed‑in tariffs, renewable portfolio standards, tax credits), provide quantitative or qualitative evidence of impact, and preferably include case studies or comparative analyses across multiple countries.

title: Grid Integration Challenges
description: What technical and policy barriers affect large‑scale integration of renewable energy into existing electricity grids?
narrative: The user wants information on both the engineering constraints (such as intermittency, storage needs, transmission bottlenecks, and grid stability) and the regulatory or market‑based obstacles (like policy incentives, grid codes, and market design) that limit the adoption of high levels of renewable generation. Relevant documents should describe these challenges, present analyses or case studies, and discuss proposed solutions or mitigation strategies.

title: Data Privacy Laws
description: What are the key differences between GDPR and CCPA regarding user consent and data breach notification?
narrative: The user wants a detailed comparative analysis of the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation and California's Consumer Privacy Act, specifically how each framework defines and requires user consent and outlines obligations for notifying authorities and affected individuals after a data breach. Relevant material must discuss the statutory language, official guidance, or scholarly commentary that directly addresses these consent and breach‑notification provisions, and should not be generic overviews of privacy or unrelated legal topics.

title: Solar Tax Credits
description: What federal tax credits are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking up-to-date information on U.S. federal incentives for homeowners who install solar photovoltaic systems, including the name of the credit, percentage of cost covered, eligibility requirements, maximum amounts, and expiration dates. Relevant documents should discuss the federal tax credit (e.g., the Investment Tax Credit), its application process, and any recent legislative changes, while excluding state‑level programs, unrelated renewable technologies, or non‑U.S. policies.

title: Solar Tax Credits
description: What federal and state tax credits are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user wants current, actionable information on financial incentives that lower the cost of installing solar panels on a private home. Content that only covers commercial solar projects, non‑solar renewable technologies, or incentives that have expired or are not applicable to residential installations should be considered non‑relevant.

title: Solar Tax Credits

title: Scope Practice Impact

narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based information on the consequences of granting dental hygienists broader clinical responsibilities (e.g., restorative procedures, diagnostic duties). title: EU Offshore Wind Policy

narrative: The user is looking for authoritative sources that describe specific European Union legislation, funding programs, and implementation strategies intended to boost offshore wind energy capacity to meet a 2030 target. Relevant documents should include official EU directives, regulatory frameworks, financial incentive schemes, and detailed implementation plans that mention quantitative goals, timelines, and expected outcomes for offshore wind projects. General renewable‑energy discussions, non‑EU policies, or documents that do not focus on offshore wind or lack concrete policy details are not relevant.

title: Rural Water Filtration
description: What low‑cost, community‑scale water filtration technologies are most effective for improving drinking water quality in remote, off‑grid villages?
narrative: The user seeks information on affordable (<$100 per household) water purification solutions that can be deployed at the village level without advanced infrastructure. Preference is given to systems that use locally sourced materials and require minimal energy; large‑scale municipal treatment plants or high‑tech industrial solutions are not relevant.

title: Electric Vehicle Incentives

narrative: The user is looking for comprehensive, up-to-date information on financial incentives for electric vehicle (EV) purchases, including federal tax credits, state rebates, and any local programs. Preference is given to sources from government agencies, reputable NGOs, or recent policy analyses (published within the last 12 months).

title: Implant Allergy Risks

narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on allergic responses associated with dental implant materials, including titanium, titanium alloys, and emerging non‑metallic options. narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on ethical oversight of AI-driven hiring tools, including academic studies, industry guidelines, regulatory proposals, and case examples that describe bias detection methods, mitigation strategies, and governance structures.
